info.name: Adjutor API Service
info.description:
Adjutor is an API-as-a-Service platform by Lendsqr, designed for lenders and fintechs looking to power their technology stack and enable critical lending capabilities.

The Adjutor API is structured around REST, featuring predictable, resource-oriented URLs. Our APIs accept JSON-encoded request bodies, return standard HTTP response codes with JSON-formatted responses, and use API key-based authentication for secure access.

With Adjutor, you can verify borrowers, score credit, automate collections, and manage both customers and loans through a unified API interface.

A test mode is also available, allowing you to simulate requests without impacting live systems or production data.

## Getting Started

Access to our APIs is easy.

1. Sign up on the [Adjutor Web App](http://app.adjutor.io).
    
2. Navigate to the App section.
    
3. Select the services you want to access.
    
4. Create your API Key.
    

## Terms of Use

To use Lendsqr Adjutor APIs, you must agree to our [terms of use](https://adjutor.io/legal/terms-of-use). These terms outline the conditions under which you may access and use our API. Please review these terms carefully before using our API. If you have any questions or concerns about these terms, please contact us at [support@lendsqr.com](https://mailto:support@lendsqr.com).

[You read about our terms here](https://adjutor.io/legal/terms-of-use).

## Getting Support

If you require assistance at any time when using this documentation or the services, please email [support@lendsqr.com](https://mailto:support@lendsqr.com) and someone would be in touch with you as soon as possible. If you are currently using Lendsqr to lend, you can also contact your account manager at [growth@lendsqr.com](https://mailto:growth@lendsqr.com).

---

# Authentication

Authentication to the Adjutor API service is performed using an API key. Every endpoint requires authentication, so each request must include the following HTTP header:

`Authorization: Bearer`

`base_url: https://adjutor.lendsqr.com/v2/`

The resources an API key can access are determined by the API scope selected during the key’s creation. You can manage your API keys and their scopes, as well as toggle between live mode and test mode, directly within the [Adjutor web app](http://app.adjutor.io). This allows you to control which resources and environments your API key accesses.

---

# Data Types

All of the API responses returned are in JSON format, with these data types defined below:

`string`  
A UTF-8 encoded string

`number`  
An integer

`datetime`  
An ISO8601 encoded DateTime. All datetimes are returned in UTC with offset +00:00

`decimal`  
Monetary values are returned with up to two decimal places and may be positive or negative (e.g., 20.78, -32.50)

---

# Response

**status** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ  
_Indicates the result of the API call. Common values are "success" or "error"_

**message** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ  
_A descriptive message about the response._

**data** ᵃʳʳᵃʸ  
An array containing various resource objects relevant to the request.

**meta** ᵒᵇʲᵉᶜᵗ  
Includes additional context or details, such as pagination info, cost of API call and remaining balance.

---

# Errors

Errors in Adjutor are communicated using standard HTTP status codes along with a JSON response body that provides additional details where available. You can generally rely on the HTTP status code alone to understand the outcome of a request.

Like many modern APIs, Adjutor follows conventional HTTP response code practices: codes in the 2xx range indicate success, codes in the 4xx range represent client errors such as missing parameters or invalid requests, and codes in the 5xx range indicate rare server-side errors.

## Error Response Fields

**error_code** ⁱⁿᵗᵉᵍᵉʳ  
A specific numeric code identifying the error type.

**message** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ  
A human-readable message as to the specifics of the problem.

## **HTTP Status Code Summary**

- `200` **OK** — _The request was successful, and everything worked as expected._
    
- `400` **Bad Request** — _The request was invalid or missing a required parameter._
    
- `401` **Unauthorized** — _No valid API key provided or authentication failed._
    
- `403` **Forbidden** — _Your API key does not have permission to perform the requested action._
    
- `404` **Not Found** — _The requested resource does not exist._
    
- `409` **Conflict** — _The request conflicts with another request, e.g, creating a user that already exists._
    
- `500`, `502`, `503`, `504` **Server Errors** — _Something went wrong on Adjutor's end._
    

# Beta or Experimental APIs

We experiment a lot at Lendsqr and some of our APIs are still under development. However, to aid, these APIs are marked with _\[BETA\]_ to denote their status. Please, do not use any of our beta APIs for production grade work as they may fail or have unexpected behaviors.

---

item.name: Customers

request.description:
Create a new customer with the provided details, including phone number, BVN, email, address, and associated documents. #### Request Payload **phone_number** \* ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ Customer's phone number in the format `2348012345678` **bvn** \* ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ This is the Bank Verification Number (BVN) of the customer. **bvn_phone_number** \* ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ This is the phone number attached to their BVN. **dob** \* ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ Date of birth in `YYYY-MM-DD` format. Must match BVN record. **email** \* ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ Email address of the customer **account_number** \* ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ Account number of the customer **bank_code** \* ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ Bank code for the account to identify the customer’s bank. Run [Get List of Banks](https://api.adjutor.io/#19ca6234-5179-4256-9c22-1103a06e172b) to see supported codes **state_id** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ ID of the state the customer resides. Run Get List of States to get list of states and their ID's **lga_id** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ ID of the local government area the customer resides. Run Get List of LGAs to get the list of lga's for specific states. **city** \* ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ Name of the city the customer resides **address** \* ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ Address of the customer. **photo_url** \* ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ URL to the customer's photo. **documents** \* ᵃʳʳᵃʸ An array of customer documents, each with a `url`, `type_id`, and `sub_type_id`. The document types supported are under the **Documents definition.** ##### Documents definition | type_id | sub_type_id | Name | Description | | --- | --- | --- | --- | | 1 | | Identification Documents | Identification Documents | | 2 | | Proof of Address | Proof of Address | | 3 | | Proof of Employment | Proof of Employment | | 4 | | Financial Documents | Financial Documents | | 6 | 1 | International Passport | International Passport | | 7 | 1 | Driver’s License | Driver’s License | | 8 | 1 | Voter’s Card | Voter’s Card | | 9 | 1 | National Identity Card | National Identity Card | | 10 | 2 | Electricity Bill | Electricity Bill | | 11 | 2 | Waste Disposal Bill | Waste Disposal Bill | | 12 | 2 | Rent Receipt | Rent Receipt | | 13 | 3 | Company ID card | Company ID card | | 14 | 3 | Letter of Employment | Letter of Employment | | 15 | 3 | Payslip | Payslip | | 16 | 4 | Bank Statement | Bank Statement | | 17 | | CAC 1 | CAC 1 | | 18 | | CAC 2 | CAC 2 | | 19 | | CAC 7 | CAC 7 | | 20 | | Certificate of Incorporation | Certificate of Incorporation | #### Response Payload The endpoint returns the details of the newly created customer with the following properties: | Properties | | --- | | **id** ⁱⁿᵗᵉᵍᵉʳ
Unique identifier for the user | | **org_id** ⁱⁿᵗᵉᵍᵉʳ
Identifier for the organization the user belongs to | | **manager_id** ⁱⁿᵗᵉᵍᵉʳ
Identifier for the user's manager, if applicable | | **office_id** ⁿᵘˡˡᵃᵇˡᵉ ⁱⁿᵗᵉᵍᵉʳ
Identifier for the user's office, if applicable | | **first_name** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
User's first name | | **last_name** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
User's last name | | **phone_number** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
User's phone number in international format | | **country** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
Country code (ISO 3-letter format) | | **language** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
Preferred language of the user | | **locale** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
Locale setting (e.g., en-US) | | **timezone** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
User's timezone (e.g., Africa/Lagos) | | **email** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
User's email address | | **email_verified** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
Indicates whether the email is verified (1 = Yes, 0 = No) | | **bvn** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
User's Bank Verification Number (BVN) with masking for security | | **id_document_id** ⁿᵘˡˡᵃᵇˡᵉ ⁱⁿᵗᵉᵍᵉʳ
Identifier for the user's ID document | | **bvn_phone_number** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
Phone number linked to the BVN | | **bvn_validation_count** ⁱⁿᵗᵉᵍᵉʳ
Number of BVN validation attempts | | **dob** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
User's date of birth in ISO 8601 format (YYYY-MM-DDTHH:mm:ss.sssZ) | | **charged_for_id_verification** ᵇᵒᵒˡᵉᵃⁿ
Indicates if the lender was charged for ID verification (1 = Yes, 0 = No) | | **photo_url** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
URL to the user's profile picture | | **address** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
User's residential address | | **apartment_type** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
Type of apartment, if specified | | **nearest_landmark** ⁿᵘˡˡᵃᵇˡᵉ ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
Nearest landmark to the user's residence | | **city** ⁿᵘˡˡᵃᵇˡᵉ ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
User's city of residence | | **state** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
User's state of residence | | **lga** ⁿᵘˡˡᵃᵇˡᵉ ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
Local Government Area (LGA) of the user | | **gender** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
User's gender | | **marital_status** ⁿᵘˡˡᵃᵇˡᵉ ⁱⁿᵗᵉᵍᵉʳ
Marital status of the user | | **rating** ⁿᵘˡˡᵃᵇˡᵉ ⁱⁿᵗᵉᵍᵉʳ
Internal rating assigned to the user | | **credit_score** ⁿᵘˡˡᵃᵇˡᵉ ⁱⁿᵗᵉᵍᵉʳ
Credit score of the user, if available. | | **risk_rating** ⁿᵘˡˡᵃᵇˡᵉ ⁱⁿᵗᵉᵍᵉʳ
Risk rating of the user | | **selfie_bvn_check** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
Status of the user's selfie-BVN check | | **selfie_id_check** ⁿᵘˡˡᵃᵇˡᵉ ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
Status of the user's selfie-ID check | | **referred_by** ⁿᵘˡˡᵃᵇˡᵉ ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
Referral source, if applicable | | **referral_code** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
User's unique referral code | | **activated** ᵇᵒᵒˡᵉᵃⁿ
Indicates if the user is activated (1 = Yes, 0 = No) | | **activated_on** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
Date and time of activation in ISO 8601 format | | **last_login_date** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
Last login timestamp in ISO 8601 format | | **blacklisted** ᵇᵒᵒˡᵉᵃⁿ
Indicates if the user is blacklisted (1 = Yes, 0 = No) | | **reason** ⁿᵘˡˡᵃᵇˡᵉ ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
Reason for blacklisting, if applicable | | **created_on** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
Timestamp of user creation in ISO 8601 format |

request.description:
Activate a customer with their unique ID after creation to enable access to loan functionalities and other associated actions.

> This should be called immediately after the customer is created.

response.name: Activate Customer

request.description:
Update the information of an existing customer using their unique ID. Only the fields provided in the request body will be updated. #### Request Payload The request body must contain one or more of the following editable fields: **first_name** \* ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ Customer's first name **last_name** \* ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ Customer's last name **email** \* ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ Email address of the customer **phone_number** \* ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ Customer's phone number in the format `2348012345678` **manager_id** \* ⁱⁿᵗᵉᵍᵉʳ ID of the assigned manager **office_id** \* ⁱⁿᵗᵉᵍᵉʳ ID of the assigned office **address** \* ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ Address of the customer. **photo_url** \* ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ URL to the customer's photo. **documents** \* ᵃʳʳᵃʸ An array of customer documents, each with a `url`, `type_id`, and `sub_type_id`. The document types supported are under the **Documents definition.** #### Response Payload The endpoint returns the details of a newly updated customer with the following properties: | Properties | | --- | | **id** ⁱⁿᵗᵉᵍᵉʳ
Unique identifier for the user | | **org_id** ⁱⁿᵗᵉᵍᵉʳ
Identifier for the organization the user belongs to | | **manager_id** ⁱⁿᵗᵉᵍᵉʳ

User's email address | | **email_verified** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ

User's Bank Verification Number (BVN) with masking for security | | **id_document_id** ⁿᵘˡˡᵃᵇˡᵉ ⁱⁿᵗᵉᵍᵉʳ

Number of BVN validation attempts | | **dob** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ

item.description: This section includes APIs related to customer management. It allows for creating, retrieving, updating, and deleting customer information, as well as managing customer profiles and documents. These APIs provide essential tools for handling customer data efficiently.

item.name: Accounts

request.description:
Retrieve a list of bank accounts associated with a specific customer by their unique ID. This endpoint is useful for displaying current bank account details on user profiles or for auditing purposes. **Response:** Returns an array of bank accounts, including details such as account number, bank code, and account name. #### Response Payload | Properties | | --- | | **banks** ᵃʳʳᵃʸ
List of bank accounts associated with the user | | **id** ⁱⁿᵗᵉᵍᵉʳ
Unique identifier for the bank record | | **org_id** ⁱⁿᵗᵉᵍᵉʳ
Organization ID associated with the bank account | | **user_id** ⁱⁿᵗᵉᵍᵉʳ
User ID associated with the bank account | | **account_number** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
Bank account number (partially masked in UI) | | **account_name** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
Name of the account holder | | **authorization_code** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
Authorization code, if applicable | | **account_type** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
Type of account (e.g., `bank`) | | **currency** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
Currency of the account (e.g., `NGN`) | | **bank_code** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
Bank's unique code identifier | | **bvn** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
Bank Verification Number (partially masked) | | **meta** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
JSON metadata related to the bank account | | **tier** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
Account tier level (if applicable) | | **is_primary** ᵇᵒᵒˡᵉᵃⁿ
Indicates if this is the primary account (1 = Yes, 0 = No) | | **created_on** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
Timestamp of account creation (YYYY-MM-DDTHH:mm:ss.sssZ) | | **created_by** ⁱⁿᵗᵉᵍᵉʳ
User ID of who created the record | | **modified_on** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
Timestamp of last**_** | | **modified_by** ⁱⁿᵗᵉᵍᵉʳ
User ID of who last modified the record | | **deleted_flag** ᵇᵒᵒˡᵉᵃⁿ
Indicates if the account is deleted (0 = No, 1 = Yes) | | **deleted_on** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
Timestamp of account deletion, if applicable | | **deleted_by** ⁱⁿᵗᵉᵍᵉʳ
User ID of who deleted the record, if applicable | #### Bank Details Object (`details`) | Property | | --- | | **id** ⁱⁿᵗᵉᵍᵉʳ
Unique identifier for the bank details record | | **base_code** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ

JSON metadata containing additional bank details | | **created_on** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ

User ID of who created the record | | **modified_on** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
Timestamp of last modification | | **modified_by** ⁱⁿᵗᵉᵍᵉʳ

Add a new bank account for a specific customer by their unique ID. - **Request:** Requires bank account details including account number, bank code, account name, and account type. - **Response:** Confirms the addition of the bank account or returns an error if the operation fails. #### Request Payload **account_number** \* ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ The user's bank account number (10 digits). **bank_code** \* ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ The code representing the bank. You can get the bank code definitions from [Get list of banks](https://api.adjutor.io/#19ca6234-5179-4256-9c22-1103a06e172b). **account_name** \* ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ The name on the bank account. **account_type** \* ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ The type of account (e.g., "bank"). #### Response Payload | Properties | | --- | | **id** ⁱⁿᵗᵉᵍᵉʳ

request.description:
Delete a specific bank account associated with a customer by their unique ID and the bank account ID.

**Response:** Confirms the deletion of the bank account or provides an error message if the deletion could not be processed.

response.name: Delete Customer Bank Account

item.description: APIs in this section manage customer bank accounts. They include endpoints for retrieving, adding, and deleting bank accounts associated with a customer. These APIs enable efficient management of customer bank account details, ensuring accurate and up-to-date information is maintained.

request.description:
Get the eligibility of a customer for a loan by scoring them against the loan product's decision model. > A decision model is a set of rules that encapsulate the risk acceptance criteria for a lender to make a loan decision. In Lendsqr, these rules or decision models are processed by our service called Oraculi. [Learn more](https://lendsqr.freshdesk.com/support/solutions/articles/44002358198-what-are-decision-models-). #### Request Payload **product_id** \* ⁱⁿᵗᵉᵍᵉʳ The ID of the loan product. **bvn** \* ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ The Bank Verification Number of the applicant. **requested_amount** \* ⁱⁿᵗᵉᵍᵉʳ The amount the user is requesting for. **location** \* ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ The applicant's location. #### Response Payload #### Top-Level Fields | Properties | | --- | | **status** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
Status of the request (e.g., "success") | | **message** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
Brief message about the request result (e.g., "Successful") | | **data** ᵒᵇʲᵉᶜᵗ
Contains the credit score details and loan profile | | **meta** ᵒᵇʲᵉᶜᵗ
Contains cost and balance information | #### `data` Object | Properties | | --- | | **credit_score** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
Calculated credit score percentage (e.g., "0%") | | **decision_data** ᵒᵇʲᵉᶜᵗ
Details about the scoring decision | | **proposed_amount** ⁱⁿᵗᵉᵍᵉʳ
Proposed loan amount (e.g., 100) | | **loan_profile** ᵒᵇʲᵉᶜᵗ
Details of the loan applicant | #### `decision_data` Object | Properties | | --- | | **pass** ᵇᵒᵒˡᵉᵃⁿ
Indicates if the applicant passed the credit evaluation | | **decision** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
Final loan eligibility decision (e.g., "Eligible") | | **engine** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
Name of the scoring engine used (e.g., "Oraculi") | | **total_weighted_score** ⁱⁿᵗᵉᵍᵉʳ
Applicant's weighted score | | **total_weight** ⁱⁿᵗᵉᵍᵉʳ
Total weight of decision factors | | **score** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
Applicant's numerical score (e.g., "0") | | **applicant_score** ⁱⁿᵗᵉᵍᵉʳ

request.description:
Book a loan using the details of the customer. #### Request Payload **bvn** \* ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ 11-digit Bank Verification Number. **requested_amount** \* ⁱⁿᵗᵉᵍᵉʳ Loan amount requested. **proposed_tenor** \* ⁱⁿᵗᵉᵍᵉʳ Duration of the loan. **proposed_tenor_period** ⁿᵘˡˡᵃᵇˡᵉ ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ Period unit for the tenor (_days, weeks, months_). **purpose** ⁿᵘˡˡᵃᵇˡᵉ ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ Reason for requesting the loan (e.g., "Home renovation"). **marital_status** ⁿᵘˡˡᵃᵇˡᵉ ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ Marital status Values supported: _Single, Married, Divorced, Widowed, Separated_ **no_of_dependent** ⁿᵘˡˡᵃᵇˡᵉ ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ Number of dependents (e.g., 0, 1, 2, etc). **type_of_residence** ⁿᵘˡˡᵃᵇˡᵉ ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ Type of residence Values supported: _Own House, Parents Apartment, Rented Apartment_ **educational_attainment** ⁿᵘˡˡᵃᵇˡᵉ ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ Highest level of education Values supported: _BSc, HND and Other Equivalents, Undergraduate, Others, Diploma/School Cert, MSc and Above_ **sector_of_employment** ⁿᵘˡˡᵃᵇˡᵉ ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ Industry of employment Values supported: _Agriculture, Banking, Education, Healthcare, Hospitality and Events, Information Technology, Law, Manufacturing and Construction, Media & Entertainment, NGO, Oil and Gas, Other Financial, Others: Public services and administration, Telecoms, Tourism & Hospitality,__Transportation & Logistics, Wholesale and Retail Trade_ **monthly_net_income** ⁿᵘˡˡᵃᵇˡᵉ ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ Monthly take-home salary. **proposed_payday** ⁿᵘˡˡᵃᵇˡᵉ ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ Proposed loan repayment start date (`YYYY-MM-DD`). **employment_status** ⁿᵘˡˡᵃᵇˡᵉ ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ Current employment status Values supported: _Employed, Self Employed, Unemployed_ **work_start_date** ⁿᵘˡˡᵃᵇˡᵉ ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ Date employment started (`YYYY-MM-DD`). **work_email** ⁿᵘˡˡᵃᵇˡᵉ ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ Work email address. Can be an empty string. **current_employer** ⁿᵘˡˡᵃᵇˡᵉ ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ Name of the current employer. **employment_category** ⁿᵘˡˡᵃᵇˡᵉ ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ Type of employment (_Artisan, Private Company, Public_, etc.). **product_id** \* ⁱⁿᵗᵉᵍᵉʳ ID of the loan product being applied for. **disburse_to** ⁿᵘˡˡᵃᵇˡᵉ ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ Where the loan should be disbursed (_bank, wallet, third-party, restrictedd-wallet_). Defaults to `bank`. [Learn more](https://lendsqr.freshdesk.com/support/solutions/articles/44002588887-how-loan-disbursement-works) **location** ⁿᵘˡˡᵃᵇˡᵉ ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ Borrower’s location (e.g., "Lagos"). Can be an empty string. #### Response Payload | Properties | | --- | | **status** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
Status of the request (_success_) | | **message** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
Message indicating the result of the request | | **data** ᵒᵇʲᵉᶜᵗ
Contains loan-related identifiers | | **data.loan_id** ⁱⁿᵗᵉᵍᵉʳ
Unique identifier for the loan | | **data.loan_profile_id** ⁱⁿᵗᵉᵍᵉʳ
Unique identifier for the loan profile |

response.name: Book Loan

request.description:
Book a loan for a customer with customized multi-tenor repayment schedules. ### **Request Payload** **bvn** \* ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ 11-digit Bank Verification Number. **requested_amount** \* ⁱⁿᵗᵉᵍᵉʳ Loan amount requested. **proposed_tenor** \* ⁱⁿᵗᵉᵍᵉʳ Duration of the loan. **proposed_tenor_period** ⁿᵘˡˡᵃᵇˡᵉ ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ Period unit for the tenor (_days, weeks, months_). **purpose** ⁿᵘˡˡᵃᵇˡᵉ ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ Reason for requesting the loan (e.g., "Home renovation"). **marital_status** ⁿᵘˡˡᵃᵇˡᵉ ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ Marital status Values supported: _Single, Married, Divorced, Widowed, Separated_ **no_of_dependent** ⁿᵘˡˡᵃᵇˡᵉ ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ Number of dependents (e.g., 0, 1, 2, etc). **type_of_residence** ⁿᵘˡˡᵃᵇˡᵉ ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ Type of residence Values supported: _Own House, Parents Apartment, Rented Apartment_ **educational_attainment** ⁿᵘˡˡᵃᵇˡᵉ ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ Highest level of education Values supported: _BSc, HND and Other Equivalents, Undergraduate, Others, Diploma/School Cert, MSc and Above_ #### **Repayment Schedules** **schedules** \* ᵃʳʳᵃʸ List of repayment schedules. **principal** \* ⁱⁿᵗᵉᵍᵉʳ Principal to be repaid for each schedule. **interest** \* ⁱⁿᵗᵉᵍᵉʳ Interest to be repaid for each schedule. **fees** ⁿᵘˡˡᵃᵇˡᵉ ⁱⁿᵗᵉᵍᵉʳ Fees to be repaid for each schedule. **status** \* ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ Status of each repayment schedule Values supported: _pending_ **due_date** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ A valid ISO 8601 date. ### **Request Payload** | Properties | | --- | | **status** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
Status of the request (_success_) | | **message** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
Message indicating the result of the request | | **data** ᵒᵇʲᵉᶜᵗ
Contains loan-related identifiers | | **data.loan_id** ⁱⁿᵗᵉᵍᵉʳ
Unique identifier for the loan | | **data.loan_profile_id** ⁱⁿᵗᵉᵍᵉʳ
Unique identifier for the loan profile |

response.name: Book Multi-tenor Loans

request.description:
Approve a loan booked for a customer using the loan's unique ID. #### Request Payload **beneficiary_id** ⁿᵘˡˡᵃᵇˡᵉ ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ Unique ID of the beneficiary. **account_number** ⁿᵘˡˡᵃᵇˡᵉ ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ Recipient's bank account number. Defaults to `""`. **bank_code** ⁿᵘˡˡᵃᵇˡᵉ ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ Code identifying the recipient's bank. Defaults to `""`. **disbursement_provider** ⁿᵘˡˡᵃᵇˡᵉ ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ Provider handling the disbursement. Defaults to `""`. **account_name** ⁿᵘˡˡᵃᵇˡᵉ ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ Name associated with the bank account. Defaults to `""`. **beneficiary_email** ⁿᵘˡˡᵃᵇˡᵉ ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ Email of the beneficiary. Defaults to `""`. **beneficiary_phone_number** ⁿᵘˡˡᵃᵇˡᵉ ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ Phone number of the beneficiary. Defaults to `""`. **bank_name** ⁿᵘˡˡᵃᵇˡᵉ ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ Name of the beneficiary’s bank. Defaults to `""`. **attributes** ᵒᵇʲᵉᶜᵗ Additional loan attributes in key-value pairs. **attributes.key1** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ Custom attribute key-value pair. **attributes.key2** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ Custom attribute key-value pair. #### Response Payload | Properties | | --- | | **status** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
Status of the request (_success_) | | **message** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
Message indicating the result of the request | | **data** ᵒᵇʲᵉᶜᵗ
Contains loan-related details. Currently empty `{}` |

request.description:
Repay a specific loan for a customer by the loan ID. #### Request Payload **amount** \* ᶠˡᵒᵃᵗ The repayment amount. **comment** ⁿᵘˡˡᵃᵇˡᵉ ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ A note or reference for the payment (e.g., "Payment for invoice #12345"). **reference** ⁿᵘˡˡᵃᵇˡᵉ ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ Unique transaction reference. **channel** \* ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ Payment method used (_bank_transfer, card, wallet, direct_debit, etc_.) > bank_transfer: repayment done externally, no debit made wallet: debits from user's wallet or virtual account card / direct_debit: debits from the linked card or direct debit source #### Response Payload | Properties | | --- | | **status** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
Status of the request (_success_) | | **message** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
Message indicating the result of the request | | **data** ᵒᵇʲᵉᶜᵗ
Contains additional response details (empty object in this case) |

item.description: This section contains APIs related to loan management. It includes endpoints for retrieving loan products, scoring loan applications, getting loan schedules, booking loans, retrieving customer loans, and repaying loans.

item.name: Wallet

request.description:
Retrieve a list of wallets created for a customer by their unique ID. #### Response Payload | Properties | | --- | | **status** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
The status of the request, typically `"success"` or `"error"` | | **message** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
A short message indicating the result of the request | | **data.wallets** ᵃʳʳᵃʸ
List of wallet objects associated with the customer | | **wallets[].id** ⁱⁿᵗᵉᵍᵉʳ
Unique identifier for the wallet | | **wallets[].org_id** ⁱⁿᵗᵉᵍᵉʳ
Organization ID associated with the wallet | | **wallets[].user_id** ⁱⁿᵗᵉᵍᵉʳ
Unique identifier for the user who owns the wallet | | **wallets[].is_admin** ⁱⁿᵗᵉᵍᵉʳ
Indicates if the user is an admin (`0` for no, `1` for yes) | | **wallets[].savings_id** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
Unique savings ID associated with the wallet | | **wallets[].account_name** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
Name on the wallet account | | **wallets[].account_no** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
Account number for the wallet (may be masked or empty) | | **wallets[].provider** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
Wallet provider (e.g., `"lendsqr"`) | | **wallets[].type** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
Wallet type, such as `"default"` or `"restricted"` | | **wallets[].currency** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
Currency of the wallet, usually `"NGN"` | | **wallets[].account_balance** ᶠˡᵒᵃᵗ
Current available balance in the wallet | | **wallets[].balance_last_updated** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
Timestamp of the last balance update | | **wallets[].is_primary** ⁱⁿᵗᵉᵍᵉʳ
Indicates if the wallet is the primary wallet (`1` for yes, `0` for no) | | **wallets[].pending_transaction** ⁱⁿᵗᵉᵍᵉʳ
Number of pending transactions in the wallet | | **wallets[].created_on** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
Timestamp of when the wallet was created | | **wallets[].modified_on** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
Timestamp of when the wallet was last modified | | **wallets[].deleted_flag** ⁱⁿᵗᵉᵍᵉʳ
Indicates if the wallet has been deleted (`0` for no, `1` for yes) | | **wallets[].deleted_on** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
Timestamp of when the wallet was deleted (if applicable) | | **wallets[].deleted_by** ⁱⁿᵗᵉᵍᵉʳ
ID of the user who deleted the wallet (if applicable) |

Name of the transaction type (e.g., `"Loan Repayment"`, `"Service Payment"`) |

item.description: This section provides APIs for managing customer wallets. It includes endpoints for retrieving wallets, retrieving wallet transactions, and transferring funds to customer wallets. These APIs enable comprehensive management of virtual wallets and transactions.

item.name: Cards

request.description:
Retrieve a list of cards associated with a specific customer by their unique ID. #### Response Payload | Properties | | --- | | **status** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
Status of the request (`success`) | | **message** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
Message indicating the result of the request | | **data** ᵒᵇʲᵉᶜᵗ
Contains the card details | | **data.cards** ᵃʳʳᵃʸ
List of saved customer cards | | **cards\[\].id** ⁱⁿᵗᵉᵍᵉʳ
Unique ID of the saved card | | **cards\[\].org_id** ⁱⁿᵗᵉᵍᵉʳ
Organization ID associated with the card | | **cards\[\].guarantor_id** ⁱⁿᵗᵉᵍᵉʳ/ⁿᵘˡˡ
ID of the guarantor, if applicable | | **cards\[\].card_owner_name** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
Name of the card owner (e.g. `"James Okonkwo"`) | | **cards\[\].card_owner_email** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
Email address of the card owner (e.g. `"james.o@example.com"`) | | **cards\[\].external_card_id** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ/ⁿᵘˡˡ
External ID of the card, if applicable | | **cards\[\].currency** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
Currency of the card (e.g. `"NGN"`) | | **cards\[\].card_type** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
Type of card (e.g. `"Mastercard Debit"`, `"Visa Credit"`) | | **cards\[\].card_last4** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
Last four digits of the card number (e.g. `"2039"`) | | **cards\[\].card_exp_month** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
Expiry month of the card (e.g. `"09"`) | | **cards\[\].card_exp_year** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
Expiry year of the card (e.g. `"2027"`) | | **cards\[\].card_bin** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
First six digits of the card BIN (e.g. `"412345"`) | | **cards\[\].bank** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
Issuing bank name (e.g. `"Zenith Bank"`) | | **cards\[\].processor** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
Payment processor handling the card (e.g. `"paystack"`) | | **cards\[\].next_retry_time** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ/ⁿᵘˡˡ
Next retry attempt for payments, if applicable | | **cards\[\].counter** ⁱⁿᵗᵉᵍᵉʳ
Number of failed payment attempts | | **cards\[\].disabled** ᵇᵒᵒˡᵉᵃⁿ
Indicates if the card is disabled (`1` for true, `0` for false) | | **cards\[\].disabled_reason** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ/ⁿᵘˡˡ
Reason the card was disabled, if applicable | | **cards\[\].disabled_date** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ/ⁿᵘˡˡ
Date when the card was disabled, if applicable | | **cards\[\].bank_code** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
Bank code of the issuing bank (e.g. `"057"`) | | **cards\[\].created_on** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
Timestamp when the card was added (e.g. `"2024-07-25T14:32:10.000Z"`) | | **cards\[\].bank_details** ᵒᵇʲᵉᶜᵗ
Detailed information about the issuing bank | | **bank_details.id** ⁱⁿᵗᵉᵍᵉʳ
Bank details ID | | **bank_details.base_code** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
Base code assigned to the bank | | **bank_details.country** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
Country code of the bank (e.g. `"NGA"`) | | **bank_details.ref_code** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
Reference code for the bank | | **bank_details.code_description** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
Full name of the bank (e.g. `"Zenith Bank"`) | | **bank_details.additional_code** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
Additional code for the bank | | **bank_details.link_id** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
Identifier linking the bank details | | **bank_details.org_id** ⁱⁿᵗᵉᵍᵉʳ/ⁿᵘˡˡ
Organization ID linked to the bank details | | **bank_details.url** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
URL of the bank's logo or other assets | | **bank_details.meta** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
JSON string containing metadata related to bank services | | **bank_details.created_on** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
Timestamp when the bank details were created |

item.name: Direct Debit

request.description:
Retrieve a list of direct debit mandates associated with a specific customer by their unique ID. #### Response Payload | Properties | | --- | | **id** ⁱⁿᵗᵉᵍᵉʳ
Unique identifier for the mandate | | **org_id** ⁱⁿᵗᵉᵍᵉʳ
Organization ID associated with the mandate | | **guarantor_id** ⁱⁿᵗᵉᵍᵉʳ
ID of the guarantor (if applicable) | | **currency** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
Currency of the mandate (e.g., `"NGN"`) | | **amount** ⁱⁿᵗᵉᵍᵉʳ
Amount for the mandate | | **actual_amount** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
Actual amount processed | | **activation_type** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
Type of activation (e.g., `"emandate"`) | | **activation_fee** ⁱⁿᵗᵉᵍᵉʳ
Fee charged for activation | | **activation_charge_attempt** ⁱⁿᵗᵉᵍᵉʳ
Number of charge attempts for activation | | **activation_fee_transaction_id** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
Transaction ID for activation fee (if applicable) | | **activation_date** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
Date when the mandate was activated | | **mandate_type** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
Type of mandate (e.g., `"NIBSS-EASYPAY"`) | | **max_no_of_debits** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
Maximum number of debits allowed | | **is_active** ᵇᵒᵒˡᵉᵃⁿ
Indicates if the mandate is active (`1` for active, `0` for inactive) | | **status** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
Current status of the mandate (e.g., `"pending"`, `"cancelled"`) | | **reference** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
Unique reference for the mandate | | **start_date** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
Start date of the mandate (ISO format) | | **end_date** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
End date of the mandate (ISO format) | | **registration_date** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
Date the mandate was registered | | **meta_data** ᵒᵇʲᵉᶜᵗ
Additional metadata (if any) | | **payer_name** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
Name of the payer | | **payer_email** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
Email address of the payer | | **payer_phone** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
Phone number of the payer | | **payer_bank_code** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
Bank code of the payer's bank | | **payer_account** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
Payer's account number | | **bank_name** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
Name of the payer's bank | | **form_link_url** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
URL to the mandate form (if available) | | **description** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
Description of the mandate purpose | | **request_id** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
Request ID associated with the mandate | | **mandate_id** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
Unique mandate identifier | | **meta** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
JSON metadata with additional mandate details | | **provider_status** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
Status from the provider's system (e.g., `"NIBSS"`) | | **channel** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
Channel through which the mandate was created | | **last_activity_date** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
Last recorded activity date | | **last_activation_charge_attempt** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
Date of the last charge attempt for activation | | **created_on** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
Timestamp of when the mandate was created | | **details.code_description** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
Description of the bank code | | **details.additional_code** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
Additional bank code information | | **details.link_id** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
Identifier for the link to mandate details | | **details.url** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
URL of the bank's logo or related document |

request.description:
Create a new direct debit mandate for a specific customer. To learn more about how NIBSS direct debit mandate work, visit [here.](https://lendsqr.freshdesk.com/support/solutions/articles/44002466904-receiving-payments-with-nibss-direct-debit) - **Request:** Requires customer details, bank account information, and mandate specifics such as amount and frequency. - **Response:** Returns the details of the newly created mandate, including its unique identifier and status. ### Request Payload **account_number** \* ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ Customer NUBAN account number to create the mandate against. **bank_code** \* ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ Bank code of the account number. [List of supported banks](https://api.adjutor.io/#acd6641b-0fa8-41a1-8ec5-68c18f4ca5f7) **amount** \* ᶠˡᵒᵃᵗ Maximum amount that can be debited on the mandate at a single time. **start_date** \* ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ Start date of the mandate in ISO format (`YYYY-MM-DD`). Mandates cannot be triggered for transactions before this date. **user_id** \* ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ Unique ID of the customer the mandate is created for. #### Response Payload | Properties | | --- | | **id** ⁱⁿᵗᵉᵍᵉʳ
Unique ID for the mandate | | **org_id** ⁱⁿᵗᵉᵍᵉʳ
Organization ID | | **user_id** ⁱⁿᵗᵉᵍᵉʳ
User ID | | **guarantor_id** ⁱⁿᵗᵉᵍᵉʳ
ID for the guarantor if the mandate is created for a guarantor | | **amount** ⁿᵘᵐᵇᵉʳ
Mandate amount | | **activation_fee** ⁿᵘᵐᵇᵉʳ
Activation fee amount | | **activation_charge_attempt** ⁱⁿᵗᵉᵍᵉʳ
Number of activation charge attempts | | **last_activation_charge_attempt** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
Last time an activation fee charge was attempted | | **activation_fee_transaction_id** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
Transaction ID for a successful activation fee charge | | **mandate_type** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
Mandate type (e.g., `"NIBSS-EASYPAY"`) | | **activation_type** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
Activation type (e.g., `"emandate"`) | | **max_no_of_debits** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
Maximum number of debits allowed | | **is_active** ⁱⁿᵗᵉᵍᵉʳ
Whether the mandate is active (`0` = inactive) | | **status** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
Mandate status (e.g., `"pending_mandate_activation"`, `"pending"`) | | **reference** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
Unique mandate reference | | **start_date** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
Start date in ISO format (`YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SS.SSSZ`) | | **end_date** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
End date in ISO format (`YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SS.SSSZ`) | | **registration_date** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
Registration date in ISO format | | **activation_date** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
Date when the mandate was activated | | **meta_data** ᵒᵇʲᵉᶜᵗ
Additional data for the mandate | | **payer_name** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
Name of the payer | | **payer_email** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
Email of the payer | | **payer_phone** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
Payer’s phone number | | **payer_bank_code** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
Payer’s bank code | | **payer_account** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
Payer’s account number | | **bank_name** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
Bank name (if available) | | **form_link_url** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
Link to the mandate form (if available) | | **description** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
Description of the mandate (e.g., `"Repayment of personal loan"`) | | **request_id** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
Unique request ID | | **mandate_id** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
Mandate ID | | **meta** ᵒᵇʲᵉᶜᵗ
Structured metadata with detailed mandate information | | **provider_status** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
Status of the mandate with the service provider | | **channel** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
Platform used to create the mandate (e.g., `"windows"`) | | **last_activity_date** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
Date of the last activity on the mandate | | **created_on** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
Timestamp of when the mandate was created | | **created_by** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
ID of the user or admin who created the mandate | | **modified_on** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
Timestamp of the last modification | | **modified_by** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
ID of the user or admin who last modified the mandate | | **deleted_flag** ⁱⁿᵗᵉᵍᵉʳ
Whether the mandate is deleted (`0` = not deleted) | | **deleted_on** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
Timestamp of deletion |

request.description:
Cancel a specific direct debit mandate for a customer by their unique ID and the mandate ID. > Canceling a direct debit mandate has significant implications. It halts all future automatic debits from the payer's account, which may impact loan repayments, subscriptions, or other scheduled payments. Lenders and service providers must be aware that a canceled mandate requires alternative payment arrangements to prevent default or service disruptions. Below is the structured response detailing the properties of a canceled mandate. #### Response Payload | Properties | | --- | | **status** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
The current status of the mandate (e.g., `"cancelled"`) | | **id** ⁱⁿᵗᵉᵍᵉʳ
Unique identifier for the mandate | | **org_id** ⁱⁿᵗᵉᵍᵉʳ
Identifier of the organization managing the mandate | | **guarantor_id** ⁱⁿᵗᵉᵍᵉʳ
Identifier of the guarantor, if applicable | | **currency** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
Currency of the mandate (e.g., `"NGN"`) | | **amount** ⁱⁿᵗᵉᵍᵉʳ
Amount authorized for debit under the mandate | | **actual_amount** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
The full actual amount agreed upon before adjustments | | **activation_type** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
Method used to activate the mandate (e.g., `"emandate"`) | | **activation_fee** ⁱⁿᵗᵉᵍᵉʳ
Fee charged for activation, if applicable | | **activation_charge_attempt** ⁱⁿᵗᵉᵍᵉʳ
Number of attempts made to charge the activation fee | | **activation_fee_transaction_id** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
Transaction ID for activation fee payment, if applicable | | **activation_date** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
Date the mandate was activated (if applicable) | | **mandate_type** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
Mandate scheme used (e.g., `"NIBSS-EASYPAY"`) | | **max_no_of_debits** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
Maximum number of debits allowed (if specified) | | **is_active** ᵇᵒᵒˡᵉᵃⁿ
Indicates if the mandate is active (`0` = inactive) | | **reference** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
Unique reference ID for the mandate | | **start_date** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
Start date of the mandate in ISO format | | **end_date** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
End date of the mandate in ISO format | | **registration_date** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
Date the mandate was registered | | **meta_data** ʲˢᵒⁿ
Additional metadata related to the mandate | | **payer_name** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
Name of the individual who authorized the mandate | | **payer_email** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
Email address of the payer | | **payer_phone** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
Contact phone number of the payer | | **payer_bank_code** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
Bank code of the payer's financial institution | | **payer_account** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
Payer’s bank account number | | **bank_name** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
Name of the payer's bank | | **form_link_url** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
URL link to the mandate form, if applicable | | **description** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
Brief description of the mandate purpose | | **request_id** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
Unique request ID associated with the mandate | | **mandate_id** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
Unique mandate ID | | **meta** ʲˢᵒⁿ
Structured metadata containing detailed mandate information | | **provider_status** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
Status of the mandate with the service provider | | **channel** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
Platform used for mandate creation (e.g., `"windows"`) | | **last_activity_date** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
Date of the last activity on the mandate | | **last_activation_charge_attempt** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
Timestamp of the last attempt to charge the activation fee | | **created_on** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
Date and time the mandate was initially created |

request.description:
Using the product ID that has been retrieved from the GET loan products endpoint, make a call to this endpoint to generate the web page URL that can be embedded within your web or mobile platform.

The request body also allows for meta fields that would prepoulate and disable these form fields within the the web page

> NOTE: All fields on the web page would be disabled except email and phone number as these details may be different on the lender's plaform. 
  

### Meta fields

**requested_amount** \* ᶠˡᵒᵃᵗ  
Amount the customer wants to check out with (BNPL) or has requested. Currency is in Naira.

**proposed_tenor** \* ⁱⁿᵗᵉᵍᵉʳ  
Duration of the loan before repayment kicks in. Must fall within the product-defined range.

**proposed_tenor_period** \* ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ  
Time unit for `proposed_tenor`  
Values supported: _days, weeks, months_

**gender** \* ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ  
Values supported: `male`, `female`

**marital_status** \* ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ  
Values supported: _married, single, divorced, separated, widowed_

**location** \* ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ  
Residential state of the user. Example: _Lagos, Oyo, etc._

**no_of_dependent** \* ⁱⁿᵗᵉᵍᵉʳ  
Number of dependents.

**type_of_residence** \* ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ  
Values supported: _own house, rented apartment, parents apartment_

**education_attainment** \* ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ  
Values supported: _MSc and above, BSc, HND and Other Equivalent, Diploma/School Cert,  
Others_

**employment_status** \* ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ  
Values supported: _employed, self employed, unemployed, retired_

**bvn** \* ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ  
User’s 11-digit Bank Verification Number

**phone_number** \* ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ  
User's phone number

**working_period** ⁱⁿᵗᵉᵍᵉʳ  
Total working experience in years.

**time_with_current_employer** ⁱⁿᵗᵉᵍᵉʳ  
Years spent with current employer.

**bvn_phone** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ  
Phone number linked to BVN

**official_email** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ  
Official email address

**personal_email** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ  
Personal email address

**amount** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ  
Amount value

Embedded loans and payments offers third-party distributors with Lendsqr the option to offer loans and payment options to customers on their platform outside Lendsqr.

item.description:
### Introduction

Borrowers and other customers generate a lot of data that are important for lenders outside of the Lendsqr ecosystem. Lendsqr allows lenders to use Adjutor APIs to get these data.

There are almost infinite limits to the data a lender can get for their customers, transactions, audit activities, etc.

This endpoint is used to obtain the current pricing of the API services. Kindly note that pricing may be different from lender to lender due to commercial negotiations that could have provide some lenders with a different pricing due to volume commitment.

If you want a cheaper price, which usually comes with a minimum monthly spend, please contact your account manager at growth@lendsqr.com.

item.description:
Bank Verification Number (BVN) is the national ID system created by Nigerian banks to uniquely identified bank customers in the Nigerian banking ecosystem.

The banking ecosystem has also created a mechanism where all the bank accounts tied to a specific BVN could be gotten.

request.description:
This endpoint is used to initiate the process for getting customer consent. The integrator is required to pass the customer's BVN and the phone number.

**If the phone number passed does not match the customer phone on record, an error is returned but with a masked collection of the correct phone and email on the customer account.**

#### Request Body

**contact** \* ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ  
The phone number or email of the customer.  
At minimum, the phone number must match the one linked to the BVN (e.g., `08012345678`, `ado@example.com`).

response.name: Initialize BVN Accounts

item.name: Complete Consent and Get BVN Accounts

request.description:
This endpoint is used to get the bank accounts. It requires the OTP that has been sent to the customer. OTP to phones are usually sent from PFAlert sender id. #### Request Body **otp** \* ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ A 6 digit password sent to the contact entered during consent intialization. #### Response Fields | Field | | --- | | **account_name** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
The name of the account holder (e.g., _ADO JOHN SULE_). | | **account_number** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
The unique number identifying the account (e.g., _1234567890_). | | **bank_name** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
The name of the bank where the account is held (e.g., _Access Bank Nigeria Plc_). You can get the list of banks on the `/banks` endpoint. | | **bank_code** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
The unique code identifying the bank (e.g., _044_). You can get the list of banks on the `/banks` endpoint. | | **account_status** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
The status of the account (e.g., _1_). Please see definition below. | | **account_designation** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
The classification of the account holder (e.g., _INDIVIDUAL_). | | **account_type** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
The type of account (e.g., _SAVINGS_). | #### Account Status - `1` → Active - `2` → Dormant - `3` → Closed - `4` → PND (Post No Debit) - `5` → PNC (Post No Credit) - `6` → Inactive - `7` → Total Freeze - `8` → Other

response.name: Complete BVN Accounts

request.description:
This endpoint allows for real-time verification of an individual's Bank Verification Number (BVN) through the comparison of their photograph and facial features with their BVN record, thereby providing an additional layer of security and accuracy in customer information validation. #### Request Body **image** \* ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ URL of the image to be used for comparison. #### Path Variables **bvn** \* BVN of the person whose face is being verified against the image. #### Response fields | Field | | --- | | **match** ᵇᵒᵒˡᵉᵃⁿ
Indicates if a match was found (e.g., _true_) | | **similarity** ᶠˡᵒᵃᵗ
The percentage probability that the images compared are similar. The higher the number, the greater the chance of similarity (e.g., _99.95_) |

response.name: Match customer BVN image

request.description:
This endpoint is used for verification of a customer's bank account. #### Request Body **account_number** \* ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ Account number to be verified. **bank_code** \* ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ Bank code associated with the account number. #### Response Fields | Field | | --- | | **bank_code** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
Central Bank of Nigeria code identifying the bank (e.g., *"058"*) | | **bank_name** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
Name of the bank (e.g., *"Example Bank Plc"*) | | **account_name** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
Name associated with the account (e.g., *"DOE JOHN"*) | | **account_number** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
Account number (e.g., *"0425571111"*) | | **bvn** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
Bank Verification Number. The middle 7 numbers are masked with 0s (e.g., *"22000000021"*) |

request.description:
Karma is a database of blacklisted bad actors within the Lendsqr ecosystem. A bad actor is someone who has been involved with fraud or has tried to request loans with fake identity. A bad actor may also be a chronic defaulter whose loan has been written off. This endpoint is used to check if a customer is on the blacklist of bad actors. #### Path Variables **identity**\* Identity of customer which could be one of the following: | Field | | --- | | **Email Address**
Format should be in the form of [email@example.com](https://mailto:email@example.com) | | **Phone Number**
Format should be in the form of _+2347012345678_ | | **Domain Name**
Format should be in the form of _example.com_ | | **BVN**
Format should be in the form of _22212345678_ | | **NUBAN Account number**
Format should be in the form of _XXX-1234567890_ Where _XXX_ is the CBN bank code _070-1234567890_ for a Fidelity Bank account. | #### Response Fields | Field | | --- | | **karma_identity** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
Unique identifier for karma (e.g., _"0zspgifzbo.ga"_) | | **amount_in_contention** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
Amount involved in contention (e.g., _"0.00"_) | | **reason** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
Reason for the karma entry, if applicable (e.g., _null_) | | **default_date** ᵈᵃᵗᵉ
Date of default (e.g., _"2020-05-18"_) | | **karma_type** ᵒᵇʲᵉᶜᵗ
Type of karma (e.g., _{"karma": "Others"}_) | | **karma_identity_type** ᵒᵇʲᵉᶜᵗ
Type of identity associated with karma (e.g., _{"identity_type": "Domain"}_) | | **reporting_entity** ᵒᵇʲᵉᶜᵗ
Entity reporting the karma (e.g., _{"name": "Blinkcash", "email": "_[support@blinkcash.ng](https://mailto:support@blinkcash.ng)_"}_) |

response.name: Check Karma for Customer

request.description:
The Lendsqr ecosystem provides an aggregated view of borrowers which lenders could have access to make decisions. To access this service, the lender must provide an evidence of customer explicit consent. Furthermore, where it is proven that a consent was never provided, the lender would be liable for all legal cost required to ameliorate issues that may arise. Lender may also be removed from the platform. This endpoint is used to verify if a borrower exists on the Lendsqr ecosystem. #### Path Variables **bvn** \* BVN of the person whose face is being verified against the image. #### Response Fields | Field | | --- | | **bvn** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ

request.description:
This endpoint allows for real-time verification of an individual's National Identification Number (NIN) , thereby providing an additional layer of security and accuracy in customer information validation. #### Path Variables **nin** \* NIN of the person to be checked. #### Response Fields | Field | | --- | | **nin** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
National Identity Number (e.g., *"32345678901"*) | | **first_name** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
First name of the individual (e.g., *"John"*) | | **middle_name** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
Middle name of the individual (e.g., *"Frank"*) | | **last_name** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
Last name of the individual (e.g., *"Doe"*) | | **dob** ᵈᵃᵗᵉ
Date of birth in ISO format (e.g., *"1997-09-10"*) | | **mobile** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
Primary phone number (e.g., *"08081234561"*) | | **registration_date** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
User NIN registration date in ISO format (e.g., *"2020-01-01"*) | | **email** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
Email address (e.g., *[johndoe@example.com](mailto:johndoe@example.com)*) | | **gender** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
Gender of the individual (e.g., *"Female"*) | | **marital_status** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
Marital status of the individual (e.g., *"Single"*) | | **state_of_residence** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
State of residence of the individual (e.g., *"Lagos"*) | | **image_url** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
NIN Image URL (e.g., *[https://documents.lendsqr.com/image_url.png](https://documents.lendsqr.com/image_url.png)*) |

item.description:
The Validation APIs are resources and tools for validating customer information. These validation processes make use of APIs that allow you to confirm the accuracy of customer information using their Bank Verification Numbers (BVNs), email addresses, or phone numbers.

The APIs are designed to help you ensure that customer information is accurate and up-to-date, providing you with a reliable and efficient method for validating customer data.

request.description:
This endpoints returns the lis of banks able to provide direct debit authorizations on their customers' accounts. #### Response Body Fields | Field | | --- | | id ⁱⁿᵗᵉᵍᵉʳ
Unique identifier for the bank. | | name ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
Name of the bank. | | bank_code ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
This is the CBN code associated with the bank. It is usually 3 digits for commercial banks and 5 digits for microfinance banks. | | institution_code ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
This is the NIBSS code associated with the bank. It is usually 6 digits long. For commercial banks, the code is in the format 0000XX where X is from 0 to 99. | | url ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
URL pointing to the bank's logo or image. |

Name of the account holder associated with the account number. | | bvn ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
Masked Bank Verification Number (BVN) associated with the account holder (e.g., "22000000022" if original is "22222222222"). | | session_id ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
Session ID or identifier associated with the account lookup operation. |

item.description:
These endpoints are used to get the list of Nigerian banks, details of specific banks. They can also be used to bank account verification.

#### Note

Not all banks are included as some banks have either not implemented their direct debit service, or they return DO NOT HONOR on direct debit requests.

If a bank is missing, please email [support@lendsqr.com](https://mailto:support@lendsqr.com) for assistance or more details.

request.description:
This endpoint allows you to create a mandate on a customer's account. The default mandate type is the emandate, which customers can easily activate by themselves. #### Request Body Fields **account_number** \* ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ Customer NUBAN account number to create the mandate against. **phone_number** \* ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ The phone number of the customer in the format `08012345678`. The customer's bank may validate this against their records, so it's best to use the number the customer receives SMS alerts on. **debit_type** \* ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ The type of debit — either `all` or `partial`. - `all`The system attempts to debit the full amount. - `partial`The system attempts to debit any available amount. Useful for loan repayments. **frequency** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ The frequency of debiting the mandate. Options: `daily`, `weekly`, or `monthly`. Required if recurring debits are being configured. **bank_id** \* ⁱⁿᵗᵉᵍᵉʳ The ID of the customer's bank, obtained from the Get Bank endpoint. **email** \* ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ Customer's email address. **start_date** \* ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ The start date of the mandate. A mandate cannot be triggered for transactions before this date. **end_date** \* ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ The end date of the mandate. The mandate expires on this date. **narration** \* ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ The narration or description of the mandate. **address** \* ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ Customer's address. **amount** \* ᶠˡᵒᵃᵗ The maximum amount that can be debited in a single attempt. The total amount debited may be a multiple of this, depending on how often the API is called. **schedule** ᵇᵒᵒˡᵉᵃⁿ Indicates whether the transaction is scheduled (`true`) or not (`false`). **type** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ Which by default is an e-mandate (`emandate`). #### Response Body | Field | | --- | | id ⁱⁿᵗᵉᵍᵉʳ
The ID of the created mandate. | | account_number ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
The account number associated with the mandate. | | reference_number ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
A reference number assigned to the mandate. | | account_name ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
The account name associated with the mandate. | | frequency ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
The frequency of the mandate (e.g., "daily", "weekly", "monthly"). | | bvn ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
The Bank Verification Number (BVN) associated with the mandate. | | phone_number ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
The phone number associated with the mandate. | | email ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
The email address associated with the mandate. | | start_date ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
The start date of the mandate. | | end_date ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
The end date of the mandate. | | narration ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
The narration or description of the mandate. | | address ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
The address associated with the mandate. | | amount ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
The amount associated with the mandate. | | status ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
The status of the mandate (e.g., "initiated", "active", "inactive"). | | workflow_status ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
The workflow status of the mandate. | | debit_type ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
The debit type of the mandate (e.g., "all" or "partial"). | | type ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
The type of the mandate (e.g., "emandate"). | | schedule ᵇᵒᵒˡᵉᵃⁿ
Indicates whether the transaction is scheduled (true) or not (false). | | created_on ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
The date and time when the mandate was created in ISO 8601 format. |

request.description:
This endpoint enables you to perform a debit on a customer's account using an authorized mandate. For this to happen, these are the prerequisites: - The mandate must have been created on the profile of that the API credentials are attached to - The mandate must have been authorized or validated by the customer's bank - The mandate start date must be in the past and the expiry date in the future For a mandate to be debited successfully: - The customer's account must be in good standing at their bank. For example, their bank account cannot be dormant - The customer's account must have enough funds to carry the debit. Mandates cannot force debit an account #### Request Body **reference_number** \* ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ The reference number assigned to the mandate. **amount** \* ᶠˡᵒᵃᵗ The amount to be debited on the mandate. #### Response Body | Field | | --- | | **status** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
The overall status of the response. | | **message** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
A message providing additional information about the response status. For example, "Mandate debited successfully". | | **status** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
The status of the specific mandate transaction. Possible values: "successful", "failed", etc. | | **amount** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
The amount debited in the mandate transaction. | | **reference** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
A unique reference identifier for the mandate transaction. |

item.description:
## Getting started Direct debit is a payment method that allows an account holder to grant authorization for a biller or lender to take money from their bank account for services as of when due. Direct debit is similar to debit cards in its ability to debit a customer’s account with prior authorization. Direct debit helps businesses that require recurring payments on specific dates with fixed amounts, such as insurance premiums, loan repayments, service subscriptions, or variable recurring payments on different dates (e.g., postpaid lines, and electricity usage). This direct debit API facilitates the process for Service Providers (referred to as Billers) to generate debit mandate instructions on their client's/customers' bank accounts for services rendered or products sold. These debit mandate instructions are created as digital versions of physical instructions duly signed by the account owners (clients/customers). Once generated, the mandate instructions are automatically sent to the bank where the account is held for review and approval. The approval process requires the bank to contact the account owner to authorize the mandate, which typically takes 24 to 48 hours. The system automatically assigns a unique mandate code to each initiated mandate. This mandate code is used to initiate a direct debit transaction on the bank account associated with the debit mandate instruction. This document provides a comprehensive overview and detailed specifications of the Direct Debit APIs, including all the necessary information for seamless integration into your respective applications. ## Direct debit process Direct debit mandates follow a streamlined process that may take at least 2 hours from activation to when they are available for debits. These steps are: - Mandate creation - Mandate activation - Setup for debit - Transactions ### Mandate creation The first step is the creation of mandate using the API defined in this collection. As soon as the mandate is created, you should inform the customer of the next steps about how to activate the mandate. From a best practices point of view, the customer should be informed on your app, by email, and SMS. ### Mandate activation Activation of the mandate is usually done by the transfer of a N50 (or N100 for banks where the minimum transfer amount is N100) to designated bank accounts operated by NIBSS. The customer has 168 hours (7 days) to send this amount if not the mandate is automatically canceled. Immediately the activation amount is received at either of the banks, the mandate is automatically activated. However, it is not available for debit at this time. #### Banks for mandate activation The following are the authorized banks which customers should use for direct debit mandate activation. | **Fidelity Bank** | **Paystack Titan** | | --- | --- | | Account: 9020025928
Bank: Fidelity Bank Plc.
You can transfer from USSD, mobile app or internet banking | Account: 9880218357
Bank: Paystack-Titan
You can transfer from your mobile app and internet banking | ### Setup for debit There are usually some backend processes done by NIBSS that then processes the accounts for debit and this may take up to 2 hours before completion. If you try to debit the mandate before this time, it would return an error message such as "do not honor".

request.description:
This endpoint is used for scoring based on the passed parameters.

The Lendsqr data provides the source of data that have been used to develop and optimize our proprietary scoring algorithms. The scoring system is continually updated to reflect new insights into the shifting borrowers’ behaviors, both for cohorts of customers and for the Nigerian financial system. The ID variable refers to the ID of the decision model.

#### Request Payload

**gender** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ  
Options include: `male`, `female`.

**marital_status** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ  
Options include: `married`, `single`, `divorced`, `separated`, `widowed`.

**age** ⁱⁿᵗᵉᵍᵉʳ  
Age of the customer (e.g., `25`, `37`).

**location** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ  
Geographical location (e.g., `Lagos`, `Ibadan`).

**no_of_dependent** ⁱⁿᵗᵉᵍᵉʳ  
Number of dependents (e.g., `0`, `5`).

**type_of_residence** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ  
Type of accommodation (e.g., `own house`, `rented apartment`, `parents apartment`).

**education_attainment** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ  
Level of education (e.g., `MSc and above`, `BSc`, `HND and Other Equivalent`, `Diploma/School Cert`, `Others`).

**employment_status** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ  
Employment status (e.g., `employed`, `self employed`, `unemployed`, `retired`).

**sector_of_employment** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ  
Industry or sector of employment (e.g., `agriculture`, `banking`, `education`, `law`).

**tier** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ  
Tier category (e.g., `tier 1`, `tier 2`).

**monthly_net_income** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ  
Income range (e.g., `700,000 - 999,999`, `above 1,000,000`).

**employment_category** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ  
Employment classification (e.g., `artisan`, `enterprise`, `private company`, `federal: public`).

**bvn** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ  
Customer’s Bank Verification Number (e.g., `22222222222`).

**phone_number** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ  
Primary phone number of the customer (e.g., `0805555555`).

**working_period** ⁱⁿᵗᵉᵍᵉʳ  
Total years of work experience (e.g., `1`).

**time_with_current_employer** ⁱⁿᵗᵉᵍᵉʳ  
Time spent with current employer in years (e.g., `1`).

**previous_lendsqr_loans** ⁱⁿᵗᵉᵍᵉʳ  
Number of past loans taken from Lendsqr (e.g., `1`).

**code** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ  
Custom or referral code (e.g., `56etruu`).

**phone** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ  
Alternate phone number (e.g., `08106666666`).

**bvn_phone** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ  
Phone number associated with BVN (e.g., `08106666666`).

**official_email** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ  
Work or company email address (e.g., `male@female.com`).

**personal_email** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ  
Personal email address (e.g., `male@female.com`).

**amount** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ  
Loan or transaction amount (e.g., `5000`).

item.description:
These APIs provide quick, easy, and cost-effective solutions for making informed decisions during your loan decision processes, allowing you to make confident and efficient choices with ease.

To use the Decisioning APIs, you must have designed your decision models and configure them within the Lendsqr admin console.

Specialized APIs tailored for Nigeria’s financial ecosystem. These endpoints support key services such as Bank ccount verification, BVN validation, NIN checks, Credit Bureau access, and decisioning tools. Built to streamline lending, KYC, and customer onboarding workflows.

request.description:
Create a new customer with the provided details, including phone number, email, name, address and associated security pin. #### Request Payload **email** \* ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ Customer's email address. **phone_number** \* ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ Customer's phone number in the format `+1XXXXXXXXXX` or `2348012345678`. **first_name** \* ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ Customer's first name. **last_name** \* ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ Customer's last name. **dob** \* ᵈᵃᵗᵉ Customer's date of birth in the format `yyyy-mm-dd`. **address** \* ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ Customer's residential address. #### Response Payload The endpoint returns the details of a newly updated customer with the following properties: | Properties | | --- | | **id** ⁱⁿᵗᵉᵍᵉʳ
Unique identifier for the user | | **org_id** ⁱⁿᵗᵉᵍᵉʳ
Identifier for the organization the user belongs to | | **manager_id** ⁱⁿᵗᵉᵍᵉʳ
Identifier for the user's manager, if applicable | | **office_id** ⁿᵘˡˡᵃᵇˡᵉ ⁱⁿᵗᵉᵍᵉʳ

item.description: This section includes APIs related to customer management. It allows for creating, retrieving, updating, and deleting customer information, as well as managing customer profiles and documents. These APIs provide essential tools for handling customer data efficiently.

item.name: Decisioning

request.description: This endpoint fetches all the decision models that have been configured for your profile. This would allow you to programmatically iterate and select.

response.name: Get Decision Models

request.description:
This endpoint is used to obtain the details of a Decision Model.

#### Path Variables

**id** \* 
The ID of the decision model.

response.name: Get Decision Model Details

request.description:
This endpoint is used for scoring based on the passed parameters.

All parameters are assigned a weight on the decision model and once this endpoint is called, the customers score is calculated according to the assigned weights.

> A decision model is a set of rules that encapsulate the risk acceptance criteria for a lender to make a loan decision. In Lendsqr, these rules or decision models are processed by our service called Oraculi. [Learn more](https://lendsqr.freshdesk.com/support/solutions/articles/44002358198-what-are-decision-models-).

response.name: Oraculli Borrower Scoring

request.description:
This endpoint enables you to create a new loan offer on the Lendsqr system. The offer generated will be based on the decision model configuration. This will also determine how much should be given to the user from the amount they requested.

> The response payload also contains a loan_token that can be used to accept the loan offer if the user is eligible

request.description:
You can accept the offer presented to the customer and create a loan request on the Lendsqr admin console using the Create Loan Request endpoint. All it requires is passing the loan token obtained from the Get Offers endpoint.

#### Request Payload

**loan_token** \* ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ  
The unique loan token received from the Get Offers endpoint representing the approved loan offer.

request.description:
Repay a specific loan for a customer by the loan ID. #### Request Payload **amount** \* ᶠˡᵒᵃᵗ The repayment amount. **comment** ⁿᵘˡˡᵃᵇˡᵉ ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ A note or reference for the payment (e.g., "Payment for invoice #12345"). **reference** ⁿᵘˡˡᵃᵇˡᵉ ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ Unique transaction reference. **channel** \* ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ Payment method used (_bank_transfer, card, wallet, direct_debit, etc_.) > bank_transfer: repayment done externally, no debit made wallet: debits from user's wallet or virtual account card / direct_debit: debits from the linked card or direct debit source #### Response Payload | Properties | | --- | | **status** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
Status of the request (_success_) | | **message** ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ
Message indicating the result of the request | | **data** ᵒᵇʲᵉᶜᵗ
Contains additional response details (empty object in this case) |

response.name: Repay Customer Loan

request.description:
Approve a loan booked for a customer using the loan's unique ID. #### Request Payload **beneficiary_id** ⁿᵘˡˡᵃᵇˡᵉ ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ Unique ID of the beneficiary. **account_number** ⁿᵘˡˡᵃᵇˡᵉ ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ Recipient's bank account number. Defaults to `""`. **bank_code** ⁿᵘˡˡᵃᵇˡᵉ ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ Code identifying the recipient's bank. Defaults to `""`. **disbursement_provider** ⁿᵘˡˡᵃᵇˡᵉ ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ Provider handling the disbursement. Defaults to `""`. **account_name** ⁿᵘˡˡᵃᵇˡᵉ ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ Name associated with the bank account. Defaults to `""`. **beneficiary_email** ⁿᵘˡˡᵃᵇˡᵉ ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ Email of the beneficiary. Defaults to `""`. **beneficiary_phone_number** ⁿᵘˡˡᵃᵇˡᵉ ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ Phone number of the beneficiary. Defaults to `""`. **bank_name** ⁿᵘˡˡᵃᵇˡᵉ ˢᵗʳⁱⁿᵍ Name of the beneficiary’s bank. item.description: This section contains APIs related to loan management. It includes endpoints for retrieving loan products, scoring loan applications, getting loan schedules, booking loans, retrieving customer loans, and repaying loans.
Specialized APIs tailored for Malawian financial ecosystem. These endpoints support key services such as loan scoring and offer generation.

This section contains country-specific API modules that expose localized functionality for financial services and identity verification. Each module includes endpoints designed to meet the regulatory, banking, and data validation requirements of the respective country, ensuring seamless integration with local systems.
